_meta.note: These tool definitions describe the contract a hosted stub would need to satisfy so a Retell agent can call it. No endpoint is live yet; nothing here has a real 'url'. The 'mapped_typescript_function' field under each tool's 'documentation' points to the Phase 1 TypeScript function that already implements the underlying logic locally (see tools/). Several of these TypeScript functions take a resolved PatientRecord rather than a raw phone number, because they were built to be composed by other TypeScript code (see bookAppointment.ts, rescheduleAppointment.ts, cancelAppointment.ts, confirmAppointment.ts). A hosted version of this tool layer would need a thin adapter in front of those functions that re-resolves the patient from phone + dob_confirmation before calling them — that adapter does not exist yet and is explicitly not built in this pass (no backend refactor). This file specifies the caller-facing (Retell-facing) contract those adapters would need to satisfy.

tools.name: lookup_patient
tools.description: Looks up a patient by phone number and confirms identity with date of birth. Must be called with both fields together before any appointment detail is discussed or changed — phone number alone is never sufficient (D1). Does not require identity verification itself to be called (it IS the verification step).

dob_confirmation.description: Caller-stated date of birth, ISO format YYYY-MM-DD.

tools.name: run_scheduling_workflow
tools.description: Runs identity verification plus the full deterministic eligibility pipeline (classification, age, insurance, provider continuity, discharge check) for a new-booking intent. Stops at the first failed check. Returns whether the caller is eligible to schedule, needs a front-desk transfer, or is ineligible with a reason. Does not search availability or book anything — call get_availability and book_appointment afterward if the outcome is eligible.

dob_confirmation.description: Caller-stated date of birth, ISO YYYY-MM-DD.

has_policy_number_confirmed.description: Whether the caller confirmed they have their insurance card / policy number available (Rule 3).

requested_provider.description: Provider the caller asked for, or null if no preference stated.

requested_day.description: Only relevant if requested_provider is "crane" — Dr. Crane himself is only bookable on Thursdays (Rule 6).

example_failure_response.reason: Riverbend Rule 3: insurance on file is not active.

example_failure_response_alt.reason: Riverbend Rule 5: switching to a different provider requires office approval first.

tools.name: get_availability
tools.description: Returns the visit duration and the soonest available slots (soonest-first) for a given provider and patient classification. Call this only after run_scheduling_workflow returns an eligible outcome, using its routed_provider and classification.

provider.description: The routed provider id from run_scheduling_workflow.

classification.description: Determines visit duration: 30 minutes for new_patient, 15 for follow_up (Rule 2).

days_to_search.description: How many calendar days forward to search. Defaults to 21.

max_slots.description: How many slots to return. Defaults to 3.

example_failure_response.note: The current implementation throws on an invalid provider id rather than returning a structured error. A hosted adapter should catch this and translate it into a tool-call error response — not yet built.

tools.name: book_appointment
tools.description: Books a new appointment for an already-verified, already-eligible patient. Should only be called after run_scheduling_workflow returned eligible and get_availability returned a slot. Always confirm the slot with the caller before calling this. Pass the confirmed slot object from get_availability in selected_slot — copy it exactly, do not reconstruct it.

phone.description: Caller's phone number (used to re-resolve the verified patient).

dob_confirmation.description: Caller-stated date of birth, ISO YYYY-MM-DD.

selected_slot.description: The exact slot the caller confirmed, copied verbatim from the get_availability response. This is the single source of truth for what gets booked — do not reconstruct any fields from the conversation. The slot object from get_availability already contains provider; copy the whole object as-is.

example_failure_response.reason: Riverbend Rule 4: patients under 18 are not seen by this practice.

documentation.adapter_note: bookAppointment.ts expects a resolved PatientRecord, not a phone number. A hosted stub would call lookupPatient.ts internally first, then pass the resolved patient into bookAppointment.ts. This adapter is not yet written.

tools.name: get_existing_appointment
tools.description: Read-only lookup of a caller's existing upcoming appointment, used silently before offering a reschedule or cancel flow so the agent knows whether one exists before asking what to change.

documentation.note: There is no dedicated getExistingAppointment TypeScript function. This Retell tool reuses the same underlying operation as confirm_appointment below — the two tool names exist for two different conversational triggers (silent pre-check vs. an explicit caller request to confirm), not because the logic differs.

tools.name: reschedule_appointment
tools.description: Reschedules an existing appointment. Requires an appointment to already exist (check with get_existing_appointment first) and requires the same eligibility pipeline as booking, since Rule 8 blocks reschedules for discharged patients too (D6). Pass the confirmed slot from get_availability in selected_slot.

selected_slot.description: The exact slot the caller confirmed, copied verbatim from the get_availability response.

example_failure_response_alt.reason: Riverbend Rule 8: patient has a "Discharged from Practice" flag; new bookings and reschedules are blocked.

documentation.adapter_note: Same phone-to-PatientRecord adapter gap as book_appointment.

tools.name: cancel_appointment
tools.description: Cancels an existing appointment. Not gated by the eligibility pipeline — a discharged patient may still cancel (D6). Only requires identity verification and an existing appointment.

documentation.adapter_note: Same phone-to-PatientRecord adapter gap as book_appointment.

tools.name: confirm_appointment
tools.description: Reads back an existing appointment's details without modifying anything (D9). Used when the caller explicitly asks to confirm their appointment.

documentation.adapter_note: Same phone-to-PatientRecord adapter gap as book_appointment.

tools.name: transfer_front_desk
tools.description: Transfers the call to the front desk. Used for billing, human requests, annual physical requests, provider-switch requests, and anything unrecognized or unsupported.

reason.description: Short reason for the transfer, for logging — not read aloud verbatim.

example_request.reason: caller asked to speak with a person

example_success_response.reason: caller asked to speak with a person

tools.name: transfer_nurse_line
tools.description: Transfers the call to the nurse line / medical professional for urgent medical concerns. Never called after the agent has attempted to give medical advice — no advice is given, ever.

reason.description: Short reason for the transfer, for logging.

example_request.reason: caller described ongoing rectal bleeding, wants clinical guidance

example_success_response.reason: caller described ongoing rectal bleeding, wants clinical guidance
